title: Board Game Burnout - And What to do About it
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_zVLb3rpe5I

Daniel (@dungeondive) opens up about serious board game burnout, a challenge amplified by the fact that content creation is half his income. Unlike passive hobbies (music, films, books), board games demand constant effort: learning new rules, mastering novel mechanics, and handling the physical setup and breakdown. Each game requires you to learn what makes it different from every other game you've played. When that burden outweighs the joy, burnout follows hard.

But Daniel can't simply step away and wait it out—YouTube rewards novelty, and viewers demand fresh content. So he's pivoting with "Project 2025," a deliberate return to his top 10 dungeon crawls and adventure games from last year. Instead of forcing himself through new releases, he'll spend time with beloved titles he knows will deliver joy. This approach addresses what makes tabletop gaming both rewarding and exhausting: the active involvement and novelty that creates pleasure can also create depletion. By choosing games he already loves, Daniel rediscovers that spark without the learning burden.

He'll revisit each game while considering relearning ease, new variants, expansion desires, and what still resonates. It's a vulnerable, honest reflection on balancing passion with sustainability when your hobby becomes professional.

----

How do you manage hobby burnout when it's something you've built an identity around?
